author: kevaundray
content: question asked by <@411317441688371202>  to <@1369712771683319879>  on the breakout call

author: dhsorens_26042
content: EVMYulLean is a strong first choice for use as a formal spec. some blockers include that it's not fully up to date. a more subtle reason is that it was a spec designed for smart contract verification and there are some aspects of evm-asm that need a more robust spec (relating to the STF/global state etc) that may not make it a very good guest program spec, as it stands

author: dhsorens_26042
content: Francesco's EL-IR is very interesting to me as a formal spec language and it might be nicer over the long run

author: dhsorens_26042
content: But EVMYulLean is by no means ruled out!

author: dhsorens_26042
content: we just want to think carefully about it, since this could end up growing into a "canonical spec" alongside the Python specs

author: frangio_
content:
thanks for the detail! i see now that EVMYulLean was designed for smart contract verification, that explains some mismatch

> since this could end up growing into a "canonical spec" alongside the Python specs
but yes, that would be great and it's why i was asking. i'm hoping we can agree on a canonical spec for everything in lean that needs evm semantics (context: i'm writing a compiler targeting evm, though i'm far from the point where i'll need this)
